name: Query and Filter CloudWatch Logs with Insights
description: Use CloudWatch Logs Insights to search, filter, and analyze log data across log groups with powerful query syntax for rapid debugging.
trendingReason: CloudWatch Logs Insights has become the go-to tool for serverless and container log analysis as Lambda and ECS adoption grows.
prerequisites: AWS CLI v2 installed and configured
Application writing logs to CloudWatch Logs

steps.title: List available log groups
steps.description: Browse available CloudWatch log groups to identify where your application or service writes its logs.
steps.tip: Use --log-group-name-prefix to filter results, for example --log-group-name-prefix /aws/lambda/ to see only Lambda function logs.

steps.title: Tail logs in real time
steps.description: Stream live log events from a log group to your terminal for real-time debugging.
steps.tip: The --since flag accepts relative times like 5m, 1h, or 1d as well as absolute ISO 8601 timestamps.

steps.title: Start a Logs Insights query
steps.description: Run a structured query to find error patterns in your logs over a specific time range.
steps.command: aws logs start-query --log-group-name /aws/lambda/my-function --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) --end-time $(date +%s) --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /ERROR/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50'
steps.warning: Logs Insights queries are asynchronous. The command returns a queryId that you use to fetch results in the next step.

steps.title: Get query results
steps.description: Retrieve the results of a previously started Logs Insights query using the query ID.
steps.tip: Check the status field in the response. If it shows 'Running', wait a few seconds and retry. Results are only available when status is 'Complete'.

steps.title: Analyze patterns with stats
steps.description: Use the stats command to aggregate log data and identify trends like error rates or latency distributions.
steps.command: aws logs start-query --log-group-name /aws/lambda/my-function --start-time $(date -d '24 hours ago' +%s) --end-time $(date +%s) --query-string 'filter @type = "REPORT" | stats avg(@duration), max(@duration), count(*) by bin(1h)'
steps.tip: Lambda REPORT lines contain execution duration, memory used, and billed duration. Use @type = REPORT to filter for these summary lines.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# CloudWatch Logs Analysis
# Usage: ./analyze-logs.sh <log-group-name> [hours-back] [filter-pattern]

set -euo pipefail

LOG_GROUP="${1:?Usage: $0 <log-group-name> [hours-back] [filter-pattern]}"
HOURS_BACK="${2:-1}"
FILTER="${3:-ERROR}"

END_TIME=$(date +%s)
START_TIME=$((END_TIME - HOURS_BACK * 3600))

echo "=== Querying $LOG_GROUP for '$FILTER' (last ${HOURS_BACK}h) ==="

QUERY_ID=$(aws logs start-query \
  --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
  --start-time "$START_TIME" \
  --end-time "$END_TIME" \
  --query-string "fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /$FILTER/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 100" \
  --output text --query 'queryId')

echo "Query ID: $QUERY_ID"
echo "Waiting for results..."

STATUS="Running"
while [ "$STATUS" = "Running" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Scheduled" ]; do
  sleep 2
  RESULT=$(aws logs get-query-results --query-id "$QUERY_ID" --output json)
  STATUS=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.status')
done

echo "Status: $STATUS"
echo ""

MATCH_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.results | length')
echo "Found $MATCH_COUNT matching log entries:"
echo ""

echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.results[] | "\(.[] | select(.field == "@timestamp") | .value)  \(.[] | select(.field == "@message") | .value)"' | head -50

faq.question: How much does CloudWatch Logs Insights cost?
faq.answer: CloudWatch Logs Insights charges $0.005 per GB of data scanned per query. Use time range filters and specific log groups to minimize scanned data and costs.

faq.question: Can I query multiple log groups at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass multiple log group names to start-query with --log-group-names (plural). You can query up to 50 log groups in a single Insights query.

faq.question: What is the difference between filter-log-events and Logs Insights?
faq.answer: filter-log-events does basic text matching and returns raw events. Logs Insights supports a full query language with aggregation, stats, parsing, and field extraction, making it far more powerful for analysis.
